design_philosophy.core_loop: Rally-based tennis competition: position player, time shot direction and power, react to opponent's return serve across turn-based volleys until someone fails to return or hits out of bounds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Timing and positioning decisions under pressure—each shot is a discrete decision point where player must choose angle and power while predicting opponent's court position
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete as Mario in a professional tennis match, mastering court control and reading opponent patterns to win singles or doubles matches
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early sports game that treats tennis as turn-based tactical positioning rather than twitch-reflex arcade action. Doubles mode forces cooperative teamwork (same-team AI partner or human co-player). Rejects 2-player competitive singles, emphasizing asymmetric match structures. Mario as default player grounds game in Nintendo IP universe rather than abstract sport simulation

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Computerized opponent AI tunable across five difficulty levels. Single-player mode allows direct difficulty adjustment for skill acquisition progression. Doubles mode reduces per-player burden by distributing court coverage between two players

progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty levels (5 tiers) unlock through gameplay progression. Doubles mode becomes accessible after single-player understanding
replayability_hooks: Five AI difficulty levels encourage incremental mastery and skill challenge
Two-player multiplayer mode extends engagement beyond single-player AI matches
Symmetric match structure (singles vs. doubles) provides game mode variety
Mario character appearance provides Nintendo franchise appeal for collection-driven players

world_building_techniques: Mario branding establishes game within Nintendo universe without explicit narrative
Court environment visuals ground game in realistic tennis setting despite abstraction

environmental_storytelling: Tennis court visual design establishes sport-specific play space and boundary rules
AI opponent behavior patterns signal skill tier (easy vs. hard opponents employ different shot selection strategies)
